title: Living in Boulder vlog 8 - 19 - 2017: 57.0 mi 5:36:53 7,500ft. Happy Bday Kirsten

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps a grueling 57-mile, 7,500-foot cycling day in Boulder, covering a broken MP3 player mid-climb, squeaky brakes fixed at Full Cycle bike shop, running out of water on the Magnolia climb, and coming home exhausted and foggy. He talks through his post-ride eating, his sleep philosophy for training days, mentions he plans to quit weed when his supply runs out, and closes with a birthday shout-out to his girlfriend Kirsten.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: sleep as long as your body needs — don't force an early wake-up if you're still depleted
eat whatever sounds good and tastes delicious; can't just load carbs because the whole body is in shock
plans to quit when current supply runs out because it kills motivation to do necessary tasks

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, where I V-Log about my biking.
Right now it's 11.27pm on August 19th, so this will be the one for August 19th.
Hello, living in Boulder, V-Log, where I V-Log about my biking.
Right now it's 11.27pm on August 19th, so this will be the one for August 19th.
Um, so today I went out on my ride at like 2 or something like that.
And I went up to, I was going to do Flagstaff and then go to the end of the road, you know.
Which went down, then I'd have to climb again and go over the hill.
But before I got to the top, my mp3 player in the middle of a song just stopped.
And I couldn't get it to just turn on again, so I thought I'd like die or something.
And so I didn't have the motivation to ride anymore and I was going to go home.
But I went to the top of the hill first.
And then I was riding down and I was riding the brakes following a bunch of cars.
And then the brakes started to squeak, like rub, even when I wasn't, didn't have it engaged.
It was making squeaking sounds, which is really annoying.
So then I just took it into the bike shop, full cycle, 18th and Pearl, and they fixed it for me.
I bought the bike there, so you just bring it in and they fix it.
And then I went up Magnolia Road, which is 14%, like I guess for a whole mile.
And, yeah, really steep.
And I'm in like, what are my gears?
They're not like, for me, I'm just cranking.
So it's really hard.
I'll probably waste a lot of energy.
But, yeah, I don't know.
Hopefully before long I'll be able to climb up it without it being a problem.
But then it zooms down the hill.
But I drank all of my water.
I had two big 24-ounce water bottles that I filled up at the bottom of the climb at M.G. Vine Park.
Because I drank my two water bottles on the climb up.
Or I drank one water bottle, I think, going up Flagstaff and stuff.
But then I drank both of them going up Magnolia.
So I had to go all the way back down to the park again in Boulder to fill them up before I went up again and climbed Sugar Love to the top, which is steep too.
So, yeah, I was pretty tired by the time I got home.
And I was kind of not, kind of cloudy in my head, you know, because I was so tired.
So I didn't do any work on my website.
I was just kind of lounging and trying to eat.
I'm still at the beginning of my writing.
So, you know, I can't just pig out and, like, load up on carbohydrates for the next day because, like, barf.
You know, because, like, my whole body is in shock, not just my legs.
So I have to just eat, you know, delicious food so that I'm craving eating.
And I love drinking fruit juice with ice at the end of a ride.
Pomegranate is my favorite.
I forgot to get that.
I got grape.
And I also got grapes, too.
But I got grape juice and then some kind of mixed berry, which is really good, sugar-free.
And, yeah, so really the only thing that I had to eat today was in the morning I had a bowl of cereal.
And then I had two energy packets on the ride.
And then I came home.
It was – I'll put the information in.
It was only, like, 57 miles, 7,500 feet, five hours, 45 minutes of riding, something like that.
So, yeah, I was – and then I came home and I ate a bunch of grapes.
I didn't finish them, but, yeah, I had a bunch of grapes and some fruit juice.
And then I had just had, like, some – a couple bowls of, like, potato salad.
No, clam chowder.
I guess I'll have, like, potato salad after.
I was going to go to Taco Bell, but they were closed at 10.
The drive-thru is only up until 12 to 12.
So, because I went to the drive-thru yesterday.
I love eating delicious food.
Taco Bell is delicious.
And, like, something like Subway.
Yeah, that's what I'm going to focus on, just the stuff that I want to eat because it's delicious.
Because food tastes even better, you know, when you're really hungry.
Yeah, that'll keep me eating.
And then the juice will keep me drinking.
And just take your time.
It's – yeah, but, I mean, I'd like to – I'm going to pretty soon get going on – keep going on my books here, finish that.
But as soon as my weed runs out, I'm going to quit, not buy anymore because I don't have the money.
Also, I'll be able to motivate to do all the things I need to do, like sell the car, you know, maybe sell the car.
So, yeah, maybe tomorrow I'll do that.
So, yeah, tomorrow is Sunday, so I'm going to try and do another big full day.
So, you know, whenever I get up – I slept in today because, like, I woke up and then I got stoned and then I fell asleep again and I got really tired, like sleepy.
And so I was like, oh, cool, let's take advantage of this.
And I just slept until I went.
My philosophy is if you're training, you know, and you feel like you don't want to get up, then sleep as long – if you can get away with it, sleep as long as you need.
And because I knew – because yesterday I felt really lethargic, like I was going to pass out.
I was, like, drowsy on the bike because I needed sleep.
I slept – I had a nap at my mom's house, but I still needed a little bit of sleep.
And so today I didn't – I was riding and I didn't feel like I needed any sleep because I didn't get up until I wasn't drowsy anymore.
And it's also awesome to just lay there when you're tired because I have insomnia.
It takes me forever to get to sleep.
Hopefully, though, with the riding.
Like a couple hours ago, I was like nine or something, I felt like I could go to sleep that and then I would have been able to get up early.
Maybe I should have done that.
Anyway, how many long time have I been talking?
I'll include a video at the end of my little video of where I went.
Okay, peace out.
I'll include a video at the end of North Boulder.
There's the, uh, reservoir.
Happy birthday to my lovely girlfriend, Kirsten.
There's Haystack Mountain.
There's my bike.
